Register your child for the club and receive a TDSRC book reporting workbook, stickers, and more to get the most out of your summer reading. Kids will be able to report 2 books per day during the book reporting times and for  every 5 books reported they will receive an entry for a grand prize ! events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This is a drop-in program for children ages 0-6. Co-sponsored with EarlyON, Malvern Family Resource Centre.

events.description: This is a free, drop-in program where children and their parents or caregivers can enjoy play-based learning, creative activities, games, and stories and songs while learning about the stages of child development. Registration required upon arrival for new clients.

Through highly engaging and catchy original songs and interactive audience participation, Mr. Eric teaches rhythm, rhyming, song writing, singing, movement, and pitch, with a big boost of vocabulary to boot! Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Harry Turns 70! Celebrating Canadian Illustrators
events.description: Harry the Dirty Dog turns 70! Celebrate 70 years of a beloved classic and the illustrations behind it. This exhibit highlights the work of Margaret Bloy Graham and her ability to bring stories to life through her artwork. Visit the Osborne collection to experience our exhibit, which also spotlights the contributions of Canadian illustrators, featuring works from artists all across the country. The exhibit can be viewed during the Osborne Collection's open hours. Located on the 4th floor of

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club - Book Reporting
events.description: June 20th - August 31st. Ages 0 - 13. Summer Reading Club is here ! events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

Plus, enter a random draw for a chance to win a book prize. *For kids ages 6 to 10. Space is limited.

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 6-12.

events.title: Snap Circuits

events.title: Make Your Own Mermaid Slime
events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Registration opens July 15.   Get ready to mix, stretch, and squish in this hands-on slime-making program where kids will learn the basics of simple science while creating their very own batch of mermaid slime to take home. We’ll explore textures, colors, and fun chemical reactions in a safe and supervised environment. All materials will be provided—just bring your curiosity and be ready to get a little messy! events.title: FINDING DORY

events.description: As Summer Reading Club winds downs, join Applefun Puppets on an exciting adventure on the high seas! In this 45-minute interactive program, join the Purple Pirate on the chase for the Great Purple Whale! After the program, participants will learn how to make a paper puppet to take home. Drop-in. No registration required. For children aged 3-12 and their families. Children under the age of 5 must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver during the program.

events.title: DIY Balloon Powered Car
events.description: We know of cars powered by fuel and electricity but what about cars powered by balloons? Join us for a fun afternoon of building balloon powered cars. Race them and see how fast they can go! For children ages 6-12. Registration required by visiting tpl.ca.

events.title: Toys Alive! Exhibition Tour
events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. From dolly’s adventures to teddy bears on the search for honey to toy soldiers on patrol. Join us for a free guided tour of TD Gallery’s current exhibition Toys Alive! Explore literary works from the late 1800s to present day from the Osborne Collection of Early Children’s Books! Registration is not required but is encouraged. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Inf

events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs.  In this lecture, we will cover: •Basics of 3D Printing •How to prepare a 3D design file for printing on a Prusa 3D printer using PrusaSlicer •How to access and use 3D printers at the Digital Innovation Hubs at the Toronto Public Library •Best practice for 3D printing and where to find designs •Review of the rules and guidelines for using 3D printers at the Toronto Public Library Note:

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

Participants will learn to type together using Tux typing which is a free and open source typing tutor created especially for children. Prerequisites: No computer experience required. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: Works by Asefeh Katoozian
events.description: Throughout the month of July, the works of artist Asefeh Katoozian are on display at Morningside branch. Asefeh is a multidisciplinary artist and architect with an interest in philosophy. Her work explores painting through rhythm, form, structure, and visual language, focusing on the relationship between composition and perception. She transforms visual elements into expressive forms that create a dialogue between nature, memory, and movement. Her practice reflects an ongoing exploration of how

events.title: Scavenger Hunt: Local Ducks
events.description: Drop by Fort York Library to find local duck species hidden around the branch and win a special 3D printed prize made in Fort York's own Digital Innovation Hub! This month's scavenger hunt features illustrations of local Toronto duck species from the Special Collections in the Arts at the Toronto Reference Library, many of which can be spotted in their full feathered glory on Toronto's waterfront.

events.title: Summer Camp for Newcomer Youth
events.description: Join our free Summer Camp for newcomer youth ages 14-18.  Participants should expect: Art and creative activities, Employment workshops, Field trips and volunteer hours.  Snacks, refreshments and Lunch are provided. Questions? Contact Nasim: 647-332-5711 or email nzand@nych.ca In partnership with Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada, North York Community House & Toronto Public Library. North York Community House is solely responsible for the protection of personal inf

events.title: Jigsaw Puzzle Social & Swap
events.description: Join us for an afternoon of communal puzzling! Bring your own puzzle or try one of the ones provided. Puzzles will be suitable for all ages. Have a puzzle you aren’t using anymore? Visit High Park's lobby Puzzle Exchange & swap it for another to take home with you.  Drop-in, no registration required. All welcome!

events.title: Sushi Making Workshop
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of sushi making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best sushi creations. Drop-in only. No registration required. Space is Limited. Ages 13 – 17

events.title: Intro to D&D for Teens
events.description: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons ! This is a learn-to-play program for T eens . 1st Level Pre-generated character available. All supplies provided. Friday(s) July 24, 2-4pm -- Adventure: The Peculiar Case of the Selptan Felines Aug 7, 2-4pm -- Adventure: Borderlands Quest: Dagger Danger Space limited, but watchers are welcome. Register online or call 416-396-3975. *Registration starts June 1.   People interested in only watching, do not need to register. Meeting Room ------ Info

events.title: Afternoon at the Movies
events.description: Just keep swimming to the movies! Dive into an ocean of fun as we reel in two fin-tastic favourites:   Finding Nemo - July 10th Finding Dory - July 24th

events.description: Join us every Friday at Eglinton Square Library for a fun family movie! Bring your friends, relax, and enjoy a great film together. - Friday, July 3 – Monsters, Inc. (2001, 1h 32m)  - Friday, July 10 – The Incredibles (2004, 1h 55m)  - Friday, July 17 – The Bad Guys (2022, 1h 40m)  - Friday, July 24 – The Bad Guys 2 (2025, 1h 44m)  - Friday, July 31 – Zootopia (2016, 1h 48m)  - Friday, August 7 – Wreck-It Ralph (2012, 1h 41m)  - Friday, August 14 – Big Hero 6 (2014,

events.title: Teen Zone: Summer Game and Chill
events.description: Teen Zone; a space for teens to hang out, play games and vibe to some music.  This session we'll have our Xbox 360 out for teens to play with friends, study space and craft space. Game list: Guitar Hero 2, Rockband, Lips (karaoke), Just Dance 3, Just Dance 2016, Dance Central , Forza Motorsport 4, Project Gotham Racing (PGR) 3, NHL 11 and 14, Lego Batman, Pure , Kinect Adventures, Kinect Sports. Bring your own Xbox 360 games as well! Crafts:  Bracelet making and origami Drop-in.

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Digital Photography I: The Basics
events.description: Come and up your photography game! Explore how to handle and shoot DSLR and mirrorless cameras while learning the basics of how to take a great photo.  Whether you just want to take better pictures on your smartphone or graduate to a professional DSLR or mirrorless camera, join us for a class on the basics of digital photography. We will go over the basic settings that go into producing a good photo and talk about camera settings, lenses, and lighting. This is a drop in program for patrons

events.title: Coding with Python

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Port Union Chess Club
events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Vision Systems in Robotics. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Saturday Movie: The Girl Who Cried Pearls and Higglety Piggley
events.description: Due to Hillcrest Library closure on July 27, this event will be postponed until January 2027. In Montreal, at the dawn of the 20th century, a poor boy falls in love with a girl whose sorrow turns into pearls. He sells them to a ruthless pawnbroker, who hungers for more. Tempted by greed, the boy must choose between love and fortune. The choice could damn his soul.
